page: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8175M processor operating at 3.1 GHz. This configuration provides 64 dedicated vCPUs derived from 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The memory subsystem consists of 256 GB of DDR4 RAM running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4 GB of memory per core. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression, falls into the lower tier. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, demonstrate average tier performance. Notably, the instance exhibits strong capabilities in large-block memory bandwidth, with read-only large-block speeds reaching 192.72 GB/sec and write large-block speeds at 90.92 GB/sec. Passmark CPU extended instructions tests also rank in the top tier, utilizing the processor's AVX and AVX-512 support.

From a resource density perspective, the m5.16xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads that require substantial memory capacity alongside multi-threaded processing without the premium of memory-optimized instances. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. The instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, whereas single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation may experience performance limitations.
description: The Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network speed of 20 Gbps. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark data shows strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and extended CPU instructions, while single-core processing and single-threaded decompression tasks fall into lower performance tiers. Multi-core operations, database tasks, and software compilation perform at average levels. The m5.16xlarge is suited for balanced multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from a stable memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8175M processor, suited for balanced multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual cores, high large-block memory bandwidth, and twenty gigabits per second network connectivity.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8175M processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4 GB per core
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and web serving
